Patch Set 1:

Alex, the functionality for verifying the parents will be implemented as part of the LFSCK Phase 2 project. There were some checks added by Li Wei to add the filter fid on objects that are missing it, but it couldn't enable the parent checks without having some ability to verify and repair the parent.
Patch Set 1: (3 inline comments)

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(3 inline comments)

Patch Set 1: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 1: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 1: (3 inline comments)

I was thinking that a good unit test for this code (layout swap + layout lock) would be to implement an "lfs swap" command (via new llapi_fd_layout_swap()/llapi_file_layout_swap() helpers that call a new LL_IOC_LOV_LAYOUT_SWAP ioctl() to call an RPC and trigger mdd_swap_layouts()) and hook this into the "lfs_migrate" script instead of using "mv".

"lfs swap" wouldn't be 100% atomic yet, since the copy + swap would not yet be safe from other thread modifying the original object during the copy, but it would at least fix the problem of preserving open file handles on the original file.

A test script for the layout lock and layout swap could look like:

        # generate 10s of verifiable IO data
        dd if=/dev/urandom of=$DIR/$tfile.orig bs=1M &
        DD_PID=$?
        sleep 10
        kill $DD_PID

        # copy the data, while swapping the layout repeatedly and
        # changing the stripe count to exercise the IO/LOV code
        dd if=$DIR/$tfile.orig of=$DIR/$tfile.copy bs=1M &
        DD_PID=$?
        sleep 0.5
        while kill -STOP $DD_PID; do
                lfs_migrate -y -c $((RANDOM % OSTCOUNT)) $DIR/$tfile.copy
                kill -CONT $DD_PID
                sleep 1
        done

        # verify that the migrated file matches the original
        # lfs_migrate itself verifies each copy after it is made
        cmp $DIR/$tfile.orig $DIR/$tfile.copy || error "compare failed"

At some later point (or immediately, if someone is ambitious) lfs_migrate should call "lfs migrate", which calls lfs_setstripe() internally to process the arguments to create the target object(s), then gets the group lock and does the data copy internally before calling llapi_fd_layout_swap() to do the swap.  At this point, the above "kill -STOP" can be replaced with "kill -0" (just to check that "dd" is still running) and should be able to migrate the file while it is being written.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 6: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(3 inline comments)

Looks good to me, although we still need to figure out what's the right way to handle this in lod_object.c. Alex, any thoughts?
Patch Set 6: (2 inline comments)

Patch Set 6: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(1 inline comment)

I thought we were going to use local logging or something else to try to keep a filesystem consistent ?

Patch Set 6: (1 inline comment)

Yes, i think we should use the same mechanism as chown, although this could be done in a separate patch, i think.

Patch Set 6:

Alex, I'm not sure what you mean when you say that the swap of the objects will cause the filesytem to become inconsistent? If the caller is verifying that the owner of the two files is the same, then the quota should be a no-op.

I don't think it would be nearly so safe to allow users to e.g. specify an exact layout when migrating objects. At least with the layout swap, we start with two files created by Lustre (and presumably consistent) and will end with two files. If the user could specify a full layout, it would be MUCH more complex to verify that the new layout was correct (i.e. using valid obejcts, not referencing the same objects twice, etc)
Patch Set 5: (6 inline comments)

Patch Set 6:

I noticed I had an unfinished inspection on the previous version of the patch.

Patch Set 6: Fails

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/7ea0f8e8-33a6-11e2-89ef-52540035b04c).  Ran 16 tests.  4 tests failed: conf-sanity, replay-single, sanityn, sanity.
Patch Set 5: (2 inline comments)

Patch Set 6:

> Alex, I'm not sure what you mean when you say that the swap of the objects will cause the filesytem to become inconsistent? If the caller is verifying that the owner of the two files is the same, then the quota should be a no-op.

I meant just this patch: it does not propagate any changes like uid/gid/parent fid.

I got to think a temporary file will be created with root (or similar) credentials,
which also mean that consequences for quota is not very clear: data removed are not counted in usage and user is able to write more data, then at some point the agent restore data with different credentials and then turn them back to user, probably exceeding quota a lot. probably this is not an issue at all.

another question was about  a way temporary files are created: how do we make sure nobody can find them?

> I don't think it would be nearly so safe to allow users to e.g. specify an exact layout when migrating objects. At least with the layout swap, we start with two files created by Lustre (and presumably consistent) and will end with two files. If the user could specify a full layout, it would be MUCH more complex to verify that the new layout was correct (i.e. using valid obejcts, not referencing the same objects twice, etc)

hmm, I did not mean to specify exact layout. at some point I suggested to use the original file (so that we go regular path and do not need layout exchange and consequences), but I guess it's too late anyway.
Patch Set 6: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 6:

For operations like migration, the temporary file could be created without a name at all, essentially just an ioctl() that returns an fd (like open() + unlink() but without inserting the name into a direcory).

Patch Set 6: (1 inline comment)

new version will be pushed
Following andreas comment, I have added a new ordering in swaping to first set a no lov EA file (if any)
I have also added tests to manage empty layout case
(I will add new sanity tests in LU-2017 for these cases)
Uploaded patch set 7.
Uploaded patch set 8.

Patch Set 8:

add layout gen swabing before using it

Patch Set 8: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

The functionality looks reasonable, but I'm not sure why the permission check for swapping layouts on files owned by the same user has not been added, or at least commented on?

This is both useful from a data security point, as well as avoiding problems with quota and UID/GID on the objects.

Also, the lmm_object_id and lmm_object_seq need to be updated in the two layouts to reflect the new parent inodes.

parents.subject: LU-2130 osp: wait until all the requests are processed

commit.subject: LU-2016 mdd: add layout swap between 2 objects
commit.message:
LU-2016 mdd: add layout swap between 2 objects

This patch add a new method in mdd to swap the layouts between
2 lustre objects.
The 2 objects have to be of the same type.

Change-Id: I26dfef2745eac67168aeceac196453c5126148fd
Signed-off-by: JC Lafoucriere <jacques-charles.lafoucriere@cea.fr>
